label: Power Yoga
description: A fast-paced, strength-driven practice that links breath to movement. Expect flowing sequences that build heat, raise the heart rate, and challenge balance and core control in equal measure.
Best suited to students who want yoga to double as a workout — sweaty, energising sessions that still leave room for a calm, seated finish.

label: Hatha Yoga
description: A slower, foundational practice that holds each posture for longer, giving you time to refine alignment and deepen the breath. Ideal ground for building strength without strain.

label: Yin Yoga
description: Long-held, passive stretches — often three to five minutes per pose — that target the connective tissue rather than the muscles. A quiet, meditative counterbalance to more active training.
Recommended on rest days, or any evening you need help switching the mind off before bed.

label: Trauma Yoga
description: A trauma-informed practice built around choice, consent, and gentle pacing. Cues focus on internal sensation over external instruction, and every posture is optional.
Led with extra care for students working through stress, grief, or past injury — no pressure to keep up, only to notice what feels safe.
